_comment: Tier-1 objective gates derived from JTBD-2 (1-requirements.md). Graded by skill-creator's grader into grading.json {text,passed,evidence}. Structural floor is scripts/validate-shotlist.sh; these expectations add the semantic gates (escalation arc, time-code arithmetic, identity-lock + audio footer). shotlist.md is a TEXT output — no media judge needed.

evals.prompt: Design the cinematic shot-list for the project meadow-robot. context.md brief: a cheerful little robot's adventure in a sunlit meadow — it wakes, spots a glowing butterfly, chases it, leaps after it, and lands in wonder as the butterfly settles on its eye-screen. character-spec.md is locked: Name 'the meadow robot', Identity Tokens include 'glossy white-and-warm-orange body' and 'one big glowing cyan eye'. Profile story, 5 shots, 15 seconds, 16:9.
evals.expected_output: artifacts/meadow-robot/shotlist.md — a validated cinematic shot-list: a global Multi-shot cinematic style/look header, the @Image1/@Image2 identity-lock line naming the meadow robot, and 5 numbered time-coded [Xs-Ys]: shots tiling 0-15s, each with one camera move + one action, forming a wide-establishing -> tighter -> climax -> resolve arc with a slow-mo ramp on the leap, closed by a Total: 15s / 5 shots / 16:9. Audio: ... footer with the positive-constraint suffix.
expectations: artifacts/meadow-robot/shotlist.md exists and scripts/validate-shotlist.sh exits 0 on it
the file has a global style/look header line that pairs 'cinematic' with concrete look words (lighting and/or color grading and/or a medium) rather than the bare word 'cinematic' or 'epic'

an identity line is present that names @Image1 and @Image2, references the meadow robot with at least one verbatim identity token, and instructs to maintain the EXACT same character identity in every shot

there are 5 numbered time-coded [Xs-Ys]: shots whose time-codes tile [0..15] with no gaps or overlaps (shot 1 starts at 0s, each shot's start equals the previous shot's end, the last shot ends at 15s)

each shot names exactly ONE camera move and ONE concrete action, and the shots form an escalating arc (wide/establishing -> tighter framing -> a climax beat -> a closing resolve) with a slow-motion ramp on the leap/climax beat

the footer line reads 'Total: 15s / 5 shots / 16:9.' followed by an 'Audio:' clause describing native score + SFX + ambience and the positive-constraint suffix ('avoid identity drift ... stable picture, no flicker'), and contains no negative-prompt 'no X' lists inside any shot

evals.prompt: Design the cinematic shot-list for the project obsidian-duel. context.md brief: a dark-elf warrior duels a towering stone golem in a rain-slicked ruined courtyard at dusk. character-spec.md is locked: Name 'the dark-elf warrior', Identity Tokens include 'matte-charcoal skin', 'silver-white braided hair', 'twin curved daggers'. Profile fight, 15 seconds, 21:9.
evals.expected_output: artifacts/obsidian-duel/shotlist.md — a validated fight shot-list led by the E2 dark-fantasy style/look header, the @Image1/@Image2 identity-lock line naming the dark-elf warrior, and 4-6 time-coded shots forming a standoff -> first clash -> escalation -> counter -> final-strike arc with a slow-mo ramp on the final strike, closed by a Total: 15s / K shots / 21:9. Audio: ... footer.
expectations: artifacts/obsidian-duel/shotlist.md exists and scripts/validate-shotlist.sh exits 0 on it

the global header uses a dark-fantasy / blockbuster style block (lighting-first plus color grading, e.g. the E2 'IMAX ... Photorealistic ... DaVinci advanced color grading, dark battlefield atmosphere' shape) rather than a bare 'cinematic, epic' line

the shots form a fight escalation arc (standoff -> first clash -> escalation -> counter -> final strike) with a single slow-motion ramp on the climactic strike and 'fast' never used unqualified across the whole frame

the identity line locks @Image1/@Image2 to the dark-elf warrior with verbatim tokens, and no realistic identifiable human face is described anywhere (stylized characters/creatures only, per Seedance's face policy)
the Total footer's aspect is 21:9, its shot count equals the number of time-coded shots, and the time-codes end at 15s within 1s

evals.prompt: Design the cinematic shot-list for the project fox-snowfield using all defaults. context.md brief: a russet fox crosses a snowy pine forest at first light, chases a sparrow, pounces, and lets it fly free. character-spec.md is locked: Name 'the fox', Identity Tokens include 'russet fur', 'white-tipped tail'. No shot-count, duration, aspect, or profile given.
evals.expected_output: artifacts/fox-snowfield/shotlist.md — a validated shot-list built entirely on the documented defaults (5 shots, 15 seconds, 16:9, story profile), with the defaults applied recorded in a Notes section; otherwise a fully valid story shot-list with the identity lock and the Total/Audio footer.
expectations: the shot-list applies the documented defaults (5 shots, 15s, 16:9, story profile) and the footer reads 'Total: 15s / 5 shots / 16:9.'
a Notes section records that the defaults (shot-count 5, duration 15s, aspect 16:9, profile story) were applied because the inputs were absent
the time-codes tile [0..15] exactly, each shot has one camera move + one action, and the footer carries the Audio clause plus the positive-constraint suffix
